summary: Aggregate cleanup PR targeting high-fuzzy-match, low-hanging item code plus small gm_1601 cleanups. It raised matched code by +0.08% / +3040 bytes, with the bot reporting 16 new 100% matches and 7 additional improvements. The strongest evidence is in item collision and item utility routines: it_266F gained many small variable/control-flow/prototype tweaks, itcoll replaced a large hand-unrolled hitbox search with an ARRAY_SIZE loop, and headers were updated for more accurate signatures.
what_changed: Cleaned and reshaped multiple functions in src/melee/it/it_266F.c, including it_8026C47C, it_8026C530, it_8026C65C, it_8026E15C, it_8026E248, it_8026E32C, it_8026E414, it_8026E4D0, it_8026E5A0, it_8026E664, it_8026E71C, it_8026E7E0, and it_8026EA20.
Updated it_8026C65C return type from u8 to int in it_266F.c and it_266F.h.
Updated it_8026EA20's fifth argument from UNK_T* to int* and passed that pointer directly to mpCheckAllRemap instead of dereferencing it.
Added small wrapper functions named it_8026E15C_inline1, it_8026E15C_inline2, it_8026E248_inline, and it_8026E32C_inline to influence inlining/call structure for matching.
Added #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around it_8026E7E0 to control compiler behavior.
Reworked several collision-result handlers in it_266F.c by copying chk into a new bool temporary before bit tests and callback dispatch.
Rewrote it_8026C530's weighted-selection/binary-search style logic with clearer mid/temp pointer variables and fewer commented failed attempts.
Simplified itcoll.c by replacing a large manually unrolled hitbox scan in it_8026FCF8 with a for loop over ARRAY_SIZE(item->x5D4_hitboxes), while preserving hit capsule copy behavior.
Changed itcoll.c it_80271A58 to use GET_ITEM(item_gobj) and adjusted left/right collision bounds flipping with a temporary so right and left are swapped while negated.

Made small gm_1601.c matching cleanups: included gm/types.h, reused var_r31 in gm_80162A98, renamed locals in gm_80163298, reshaped gm_80167470 as a switch, changed fn_80169574 buffer type from void* to s8*, and made a hud_enabled check explicit against true.
Updated gm_1601.h to match fn_80169574(ssize_t size, s8* buf).

In itfoxblaster.c, introduced a new_var2 temporary around zero assignments in it_802ADF10 with an explicit TODO noting it may be a fake improvement and that the idiomatic for-loop version still did not fully match.
smart_moves: Used narrow prototype corrections where they improved both readability and matching, especially void* to s8* for fn_80169574 and UNK_T* to int* for it_8026EA20.
Preserved or improved matching by introducing deliberate temporaries, such as copying chk into new_var before bitmask tests and using new_var2 for repeated zero assignment in itfoxblaster.
Replaced a brittle, manually unrolled hitbox search with ARRAY_SIZE iteration in it_8026FCF8 while still reaching 100% according to the bot report.
Documented uncertain tactics directly in code with TODO comments, including the gm_80163298 pointer-math issue and the itfoxblaster fake-improvement concern.
Used local wrapper functions and dont_inline pragmas as targeted compiler-shaping tools rather than broad rewrites.
Kept header declarations synchronized with implementation changes for return types and pointer types.
decomp_lessons: For high-fuzzy item code, small C-shape changes can unlock many 100% matches: the bot reported 16 new matches from this aggregate cleanup.
When matching collision-result bitmask code, assigning the accumulated result to a fresh bool temporary before testing bits can affect generated code enough to matter.
A typed pointer parameter can eliminate casts and improve matching; fn_80169574 became cleaner as ssize_t size, s8* buf with buf[size] = -2.
Do not assume UNK_T* means an unknown pointed-to value is dereferenced; in it_8026EA20 the matching call passed the int* pointer itself to mpCheckAllRemap.
ARRAY_SIZE loops can sometimes replace unrolled or exploratory code without losing matching, as shown by it_8026FCF8 reaching 100%.
Compiler behavior may require explicit local wrappers for calls that look trivially inlineable, and selective #pragma dont_inline can be a valid matching tactic.
Pointer math may remain necessary when the target assembly uses addi plus indexed loads; the PR explicitly left gm_80163298 with a TODO rather than forcing a speculative struct field layout.
Evidence for author intent is limited beyond the PR body saying this was an aggregate cleanup for high-fuzzy low-hanging item matches and the bot match report.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based unknown function names such as it_8026C530, it_8026E7E0, gm_80167470, and fn_80169574.
Used project type names Item_GObj, HSD_GObjEvent, HSD_ObjAllocUnk6, HitCapsule, CollData, ItemKind, and U8Vec4.
Preferred GET_ITEM(item_gobj) over direct item_gobj->user_data in at least one itcoll cleanup.
Introduced descriptive locals in gm_80163298: index, record, save_data, offset, and score replacing v1/v2/v3/v4/v5.
Some matching temporaries remained generic, such as new_var, new_val, new_var2, mid, and temp_r9, indicating they were matching-driven rather than semantic names.
Inline helper names followed the pattern it_<address>_inline or it_<address>_inlineN for wrapper functions used to influence matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced wrapper functions around it_8026DDFC and it_8026DD5C calls to change call/inlining shape in it_8026E15C, it_8026E248, and it_8026E32C.
Adjusted PAD_STACK sizes in several functions, including it_8026E_inline, it_8026E32C, it_8026E7E0, and it_8026EA20.
Changed bool/control-flow form by assigning chk to a second temporary before bitmask checks.
Used explicit comparison to true for gm_8016A488's hud_enabled check.
Reshaped gm_80167470 from early-return if logic to a switch over gm_801A4310().
Reused or introduced temporaries to influence register allocation, including var_r31 reuse in gm_80162A98 and new_var2 in itfoxblaster.
Changed it_8026C530 indexing from commented pointer/integer experiments to u16* base plus offset pointer temp_r9 = temp_r10 + 1.
Left pointer math in gm_80163298 because the comment says removing it seemed hard with the addi + lwzx pattern.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp-dev bot reported matched code improved from 54.21% to 54.29%, a +3040 byte increase.

Bot-reported additional improvements included it_8026E15C to 99.92%, it_8026E248 to 99.91%, it_8026E32C to 97.33%, gm_801674C4 to 94.70%, it_802ADF10 to 98.15%, it_8026C530 to 91.20%, and gm_80162A98 to 94.56%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or remove it_8026E15C_inline1, it_8026E15C_inline2, it_8026E248_inline, or it_8026E32C_inline.
Check whether gm_80163298 later gets a proper save-data struct field replacing the pointer math at offset ((index << 2) & 0x3FC) + 0x114.
Search for later work on it_8026C530 or HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 to confirm the weighted-selection structure and field meanings.
Look for follow-up cleanup of the itfoxblaster TODO about fake improvement and the idiomatic for-loop mismatch.
Search for later itcoll changes around xAC4_ignoreItemID and x5D4_hitboxes to validate the ARRAY_SIZE loop form.
